question: Why did profit drop this month?
summary: Profit softened because fixed overhead rose while a few chronic-care SKUs sold with lower realized margin, and one upcoming distributor payment is compressing short-term cash.
risks: Margin can slip further if slow-moving stock is not rebalanced
Cash buffer narrows if recurring bills and supplier payments land together
recommendations: Trim June reorder quantities on slow dermatology SKUs
Prioritize near-expiry stock in the next clinic outreach cycle
Delay one discretionary purchase until after the next payment weekend
